heading: Safety assurance review
label: 2344 Safety assurance review
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Water Resources Development Act of 2007, and not as part of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 which comprises this chapter.

Amendments2022—Subsec. (g). substituted “chapter 10 of title 5” for “FACA” in heading and “Chapter 10 of title 5” for “The Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.)” in text.Pub. L. 117–2862014—Subsec. (g). added subsec. (g).Pub. L. 113–121
“Secretary” DefinedSecretary means the Secretary of the Army, see , set out as a note under .section 2 of Pub. L. 110–114section 2201 of this title
amendments: Amendments2022—Subsec. (g). substituted “chapter 10 of title 5” for “FACA” in heading and “Chapter 10 of title 5” for “The Federal Advisory Committee Act (5 U.S.C. App.)” in text.Pub. L. 117–2862014—Subsec. (g). added subsec. (g).Pub. L. 113–121

children.heading: Projects subject to safety assurance review
children.label: a Projects subject to safety assurance review
children.text: The Chief of Engineers shall ensure that the design and construction activities for hurricane and storm damage reduction and flood damage reduction projects are reviewed by independent experts under this section if the Chief of Engineers determines that a review by independent experts is necessary to assure public health, safety, and welfare.
the failure of the project would pose a significant threat to human life;
the project involves the use of innovative materials or techniques;
the project design lacks redundancy; or
the project has a unique construction sequencing or a reduced or overlapping design construction schedule.

children.heading: Initiation of review
children.label: 1 Initiation of review
children.text: At the appropriate point in the development of detailed engineering and design specifications for each water resources project subject to review under this section, the Chief of Engineers shall initiate a safety assurance review by independent experts on the design and construction activities for the project.

children.heading: Selection of reviewers
children.label: 2 Selection of reviewers
children.text: A safety assurance review under this section shall include participation by experts selected by the Chief of Engineers from among individuals who are distinguished experts in engineering, hydrology, or other appropriate disciplines. The Chief of Engineers shall apply the National Academy of Science’s policy for selecting reviewers to ensure that reviewers have no conflict of interest with the project being reviewed.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: 3 Compensation
children.text: An individual serving as an independent reviewer under this section shall be compensated at a rate of pay to be determined by the Secretary and shall be allowed travel expenses.

children.heading: Scope of safety assurance reviews
children.label: d Scope of safety assurance reviews
children.text: A safety assurance review under this section shall include a review of the design and construction activities prior to the initiation of physical construction and periodically thereafter until construction activities are completed on a regular schedule sufficient to inform the Chief of Engineers on the adequacy, appropriateness, and acceptability of the design and construction activities for the purpose of assuring public health, safety, and welfare. The Chief of Engineers shall ensure that reviews under this section do not create any unnecessary delays in design and construction activities.

children.heading: Safety assurance review record
children.label: e Safety assurance review record
children.text: The written recommendations of a reviewer or panel of reviewers under this section and the responses of the Chief of Engineers shall be available to the public, including through electronic means on the Internet.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: f Applicability
children.text: This section shall apply to any project in design or under construction on , and to any project with respect to which design or construction is initiated during the period beginning on , and ending 7 years after .November 8, 2007November 8, 2007November 8, 2007

children.heading: Nonapplicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: g Nonapplicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5 shall not apply to a safety assurance review conducted under this section.
